channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A private European aerospace startup completes the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle
item.description: A rocket by a private European aerospace company launched from Norway on Sunday and crashed into the sea 30 seconds later.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How to sync iPhone and iPad – iCloud, Photos, Calendars, and more
item.description: One of the big perks of buying an Apple product is the iCloud ecosystem, which keeps everything updated and connected across your devices – here’s how to sync your iPhone and iPad.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: iMessage still lags behind its peers. 4 ways Apple should update it for iOS 19
item.description: Few apps are as inextricably linked to the iPhone as Apple’s Messages. Introduced with the original iPhone almost 18 years ago, the app (then called “Text”) has become the primary messenger for most iPhone users worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: New 3D technology could soon bring surgeons closer to patients in Africa's most remote regions
item.description: A groundbreaking initiative could soon be bringing surgeons closer to patients in Africa's remote corners where the doctor-to-patients ratio is among the lowest in the world.
item.dc:creator: Naa Adorkor Cudjoe, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: OpenAI’s Studio Ghibli-style images renew the debate over AI and copyright
item.description: This week, the internet had a rare opportunity to transform its selfies and family photos into stunning Studio Ghibli “portraits.” What started as a lighthearted trend quickly took a darker turn.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: ECMAScript 2025 JavaScript standard takes shape
item.description: ECMAScript 2025, the next version of an ECMA International standard for JavaScript, will standardize new JavaScript capabilities ranging from JSON modules to import attributes, new Set methods, sync iterator helpers, and regular expression modifiers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How the Navy is removing barriers to IT modernization
item.description: Barry Tanner, the deputy CIO for the Department of the Navy, said the initiative to reduce and eliminate duplicative applications is targeting business systems. The post How the Navy is removing barriers to IT modernization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Group chats rule the world
item.description: How many group chats are you involved in? For me, it varies. Right now, there are only two — and both are of a more private nature.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why the ‘iPhone of smartwatches’ remains so elusive
item.description: Ever since it became obvious that the iPhone was one of the most transformative consumer products in history, a question has been floating out there: Would its impact ever be matched by a device in an even newer category? And if something pulled it off, what would it be? After a decade and a half—so
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Convicted of bilking investors, Nikola founder and Trump donor gets a presidential pardon
item.description: The founder of an electric car start-up sentenced to prison for exaggerating the potential of his technology has been pardoned by President Donald Trump, potentially wiping out hundreds of millions of dollars in restitution that prosecutors were seeking for bamboozled investors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk: Trump will 'go after' people 'pushing the lies' about Tesla
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk suggested Thursday that the Trump administration will crack down on vehicle vandalization, expressing his concerns about protesters targeting Tesla dealerships across the U.S. and abroad.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: La transformación digital, esencial para la supervivencia del sector asegurador
item.description: Los beneficios de la transformación digital ya son palpables para el sector asegurador. Así lo constata el informe Perspectivas de tecnología en seguros 2025: transformando la innovación en ventaja competitiva que firma Concentrix.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI culture war: Hidden bias in training models may push political propaganda
item.description: The release of Chinese-made DeepSeek has generated heated debate, but critics have largely ignored a huge problem: the potential for China to use such an AI model to push a cultural and political agenda on the rest of the world.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Senate Republican: Taylor Swift could write song about 'terrible breakup' between Musk, liberals
item.description: Sen. Bernie Moreno (R-Ohio) joked Thursday about the “terrible breakup” between Elon Musk and liberals, as Democrats denounce the tech billionaire’s prominent role in the Trump administration. After Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Qualcomm launches global antitrust offensive against Arm, accusing it of stifling competition
item.description: Qualcomm has launched a global antitrust offensive against Arm Holdings, accusing its longtime partner of anti-competitive practices in regulatory complaints filed across three continents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How RamaLama helps make AI model testing safer
item.description: Consider this example: An amazing new software tool emerges suddenly, turning technology industry expectations on their heads by delivering unprecedented performance at a fraction of the existing cost.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump floats reducing tariffs on China to secure TikTok deal
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday floated reducing tariffs on China to get a TikTok divestiture deal done as the April 5 deadline nears.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Federal News Network’s CX Exchange 2025
item.description: End-to-End Experience – The evolution of federal service delivery The post Federal News Network’s CX Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: The Apple rumor machine cranks into gear for iOS 19
item.description: WWDC event marketing intensifies, and as we head toward that event in a little over two months, it looks as if we’re being told to expect a new paint job (aka user interface change)s for iOS.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Critical RCE flaws put Kubernetes clusters at risk of takeover
item.description: The Kubernetes project has released patches for five vulnerabilities in a widely used popular component called the Ingress NGINX Controller that’s used to route external traffic to Kubernetes services. If exploited, the flaw could allow attackers to completely take over entire clusters.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Ethernet roadmap: AI drives high-speed, efficient Ethernet networks
item.description: At next month’s Optical Fiber Communication Conference and Exhibition (OFC), many of the technologies that are driving current and future Ethernet development will be on full display by the Ethernet Alliance, which is set to unveil its tenth anniversary Ethernet roadmap.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft’s newest AI agents can detail how they reason
item.description: If you’re wondering how AI agents work, Microsoft’s new Copilot AI agents provide real-time answers on how data is being analyzed and sourced to reach results.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
